tax: Sump Surger is placed at species rank beneath Surger (Fluvial order, Water Elementals class). Diagnosis rests on a self-cohering liquid-water column, a directed drowning surge, and collapse to a flat flood when dispersed. It is separated from its sibling the Cataract Spume by its dark still-water surge from depth versus the Spume's aerated white-water churn from falls; the two share the genus body-plan but never the aerated white water. Composition, not colour or menace, fixes the placement: still sump-water held coherent by water-pressure animation.
ap: Sump Surger stands 2–3 m column, tonnes of water. Its silhouette is an upright water column that rears and curls into a breaking wave. Three diagnostic surface markers separate it in the field: a column of water standing where water should pool, with a slick coherent skin; silt and debris suspended in a fixed pattern rather than settling; and a low surge-sound and a pressure-swell felt through the floor before it rises. It moves with a gliding surge across wet floor, fastest toward and through standing water, and shows none; dark and reflective. Even after dispersal it can be identified from a high-water mark, scattered silt, and a drained sump where it drew its mass, so a survey team need not see the living body to log the species.
eco: Sump Surger persists in flooded sumps, sump-chambers, and standing pools of the lower tiers, where a body of water is always to hand. It does not breed; it forms where water-pressure animates a flooded sump into a coherent column, and recurs whenever the sump refills and the next pressure-pulse raises it again. It sustains its mass on water-pressure in flooded chambers, drawing in water drawn from its sump to replace what it sheds. Range is bounded by the wetted, water-supplied zone: outside that band the body collapses into a flat flood on dry, draining ground. Juvenile or nascent forms appear as a sump whose surface stands in a low standing wave without any wind, which lets a surveyor predict where an adult will rise.
beh: Behaviour is the behaviour of its medium given intent. Sump Surger is roused by entry into its water or heavy footfall on its wet floor and otherwise lies a still flooded pool indistinguishable from any sump. It claims a single sump-chamber and answers intrusion with rearing and breaking over the intruder to drown and sweep. Toward grey-folk it is reactive to anything entering its water; survey crews report that Stillwardens sound a sump's depth from dry stone, never wade an unsounded pool. It is not cultural and keeps no craft — its 'decisions' are thresholds of pressure, heat, current, or charge, not plans.
traitsText: Stable characters of Sump Surger: a body of still sump-water held coherent by water-pressure animation; immune to piercing, slashing, and cutting, which pass through it; and a defining weakness — freezing and absorbent earth, which lock or drain its body. Its senses run through the medium itself: pressure and motion sensing through the water; blind to dry, still objects. It tolerates darkness, depth, and cold short of freezing but fails at freezing and draining onto absorbent ground. Mass varies with available element, yet the proportions, the coherent column and breaking-surge form, stay constant, which is why mass alone never reclassifies the taxon.
abilities: Abilities of Sump Surger are bounded by its anatomy, not borrowed from other elements: it surges to engulf and drown prey; it sweeps footing out from under crews with a directed wave; it draws water from its sump to repair and grow; and it sinks into the pool to ambush and resurge. Each is a direct expression of water-pressure animating still water, so it cannot, for instance, produce heat, hold form when frozen, or cross dry absorbent ground. The powers scale with body mass and fade as the body disperses.
bg: In the Throat, Sump Surger matters because it makes every unsounded sump a drowning trap, and crews now sound depth from dry stone before wading. Grey-folk survey practice treats it as a drowning hazard logged with sumps and standing pools, and its presence reshapes routes through the lower-tier sumps, now sounded from dry stone and bridged rather than waded. A sump surface standing in a windless standing wave is the Stillwardens' sign a Surger has formed.
g: Sump Surger (Unda profunda) is a liquid-water elemental that rises as a coherent column from a flooded sump and drowns prey in a directed surge. Documented from the flooded sumps and sump-chambers of the lower tiers, it expresses a single inner element as its whole anatomy — a self-cohering column of dark water that holds its shape against gravity — rather than as flesh laid over an animal skeleton. It carries no solid part; its body is water given will, holding a column where water should spread and fall. Field teams record it as a true elemental, not a beast wearing mineral armour: when it disperses it leaves a sudden flat flood across the chamber floor and a draining gurgle and no carcass.
